notes:
- Key concepts introduced (with brief explanation)
  - Human-in-the-loop (two modes)
    - Pre-answer approval: human is asked for feedback/approval before the agent returns final output (the agent pauses mid-flow to ask).
    - Post-answer feedback: user provides feedback after the agent completes its run; the team can pick up that feedback on the next run because it retains state.
  - Agent teams / roles
    - Multiple assistants defined with distinct roles (example: writer, reviewer, editor) working as a team.
  - Team loop and task override
    - Team runs inside a loop; initial task provided once, then can be overridden (e.g., set to "feedback") so subsequent runs use prior context.
  - Max turns
    - Team parameter controlling how many turns agents take (example: max_turns=3). Can be set to 1 to request feedback after every run.
    - Note: max_turns is specific to Team class and currently supported only by roundrobin selector, group chat, and swarm when used with a termination condition.
  - Termination condition
    - Mechanism to stop the loop based on message type or other criteria (e.g., message type == "top result").
  - Persistent team state
    - Team must maintain state (conversation/context) for iterative feedback to be meaningful; resetting team clears context.
    - save_state function available on agents and team to persist to file or database and load on startup.
    - Use-case: preserve per-user or per-application team context so each user’s team continues asynchronously with their context.
  - Tools in AutoGen / agent frameworks
    - Tools are executable code the agent calls (functions or APIs) in response to model-generated function calls.
    - Model-generated function call flow: model decides to call a tool -> tool executed with focused inputs (not full query) -> tool returns result -> agent returns result to user.
    - Categories: author mentions three major categories (autogen treats two combined), and emphasizes one category now:
      - Custom function tool: user-provided functions wrapped as tools (example: reverse_string).
    - FunctionTool wrapper
      - Recommended wrapper that uses description and type annotations to inform the LLM when and how to use the function.
    - Best practices for tools: provide types (input/output), docstrings/descriptions so the LLM can make better calls.
  - Example flows / behaviors
    - Team is not waiting for user (autonomous execution like ChatGPT background behavior).
    - Tool example flow logged: initial text message -> tool call request (only required part) -> tool response (same ID) -> agent returns output.

- Any analogies or metaphors used
  - Tools = "special powers" that make agents stronger.
  - Tools supercharge agents like laptops/apps/calculators supercharge human workflows.
  - Team behavior compared to ChatGPT having people in an office approving responses (human-in-the-loop approval metaphor).

- Any code, commands, or config shown (captured as referenced items/parameters)
  - agent definitions: assistant1, assistant2, assistant3 (roles: writer, reviewer, editor)
  - team config: max_turns = 3 (example); can set to 1.
  - Loop control: overriding initial task to "feedback"; user prompt "Type exit to stop feedback. Strip exit."
  - Example user interactions referenced: "write threeline poem", "make it a little longer with dragon", "write exit"
  - Team control methods:
    - team.reset() (implied by "team reset" clears context)
    - save_state() on agents and on team (persist state to file or DB)
    - load state on startup (load persisted state for a user/application)
  - Function/tool example:
    - reverse_string function (simple tool example)
    - FunctionTool wrapper: uses description and type annotations (input: string, output: string, docstring "the reversed string is returned")
  - Implementation notes:
    - FunctionTool helps LLM determine when/how to call a function via description + type annotations.
    - When the model makes a function call, it will send only the relevant part of the query to the tool (not the entire user content).

- Any warnings, gotchas, or important caveats
  - Team state must be maintained; if you reset the team you lose context and feedback becomes meaningless.
  - Persist the team/agent state externally (file or database) if used in applications where sessions may restart.
  - Documentation may be incomplete / "wavy" — expect to read core code or experiment.
  - max_turns support is limited: only works with Team class and only supported by roundrobin selector, group chat, and swarm when used with termination condition.
  - The team runs autonomously and may not wait for a user input unless you design it to pause (human-in-loop design choice).
  - Best practice: always include type annotations and docstrings for tools so LLMs can call them accurately.

- Any comparisons or lists (potential table candidates)
  - Two human-in-the-loop approaches
    - Pre-output approve (pause inside generation)
    - Post-output feedback (provide feedback after run; team uses saved context next time)
  - Tool categories (broad)
    - Custom function tool (user-defined functions)
    - Other categories implied (autogen merges two into one) — three major categories overall (not fully enumerated here)
  - Supported selectors for max_turns + termination condition
    - roundrobin selector
    - group chat
    - swarm
  - Typical team roles example
    - Assistant 1: Writer
    - Assistant 2: Reviewer
    - Assistant 3: Editor
  - Tool call flow steps
    - User initial message -> model generates function call -> tool executes -> tool responds -> agent returns final output

- Direct quotes worth preserving
  - "Type exit to stop feedback. Strip exit."
  - "the team should maintain the state."
  - "save_state function on the agents as well as on the team."
  - "tools are code that can be executed by an agent or perform action."
  - "A tool can be simple function as an calculator or an API call to a third party service such as stock price lookup."
  - "function tool class uses description and type annotations to inform the LLM when and how to use a given function."
  - "tools are like special powers"
  - "this is the way it runs"
  - "max turn is specific to team class and is currently only supported by roundroin selector group chat and swarm when used with termination condition."
